name: Two Rocks Reef
notes: Limestone reef system with scattered ledges; productive for demersal species on the drop at dawn and dusk.

name: Alkimos Reef
notes: Popular nearshore reef with gutters and ledges. Good tailor fishing along the surface structure at dusk.

name: Marmion Reef
notes: Part of the Marmion Marine Park. Limestone reef with extensive cave and ledge habitat; one of Perth's most fished reefs.

name: Whitfords Reef
notes: Shallow inshore reef within the Marmion Marine Park. Good for baldchin groper along rubble edges.

name: Rottnest Island (North Reef)
notes: Dynamic reef system on the exposed north side of Rottnest. Strong currents attract pelagics; excellent dhufish on the deeper ledges.

name: Rottnest Island (South Side)
notes: Sheltered south-side reefs with complex ledge and gutter habitat. Key dhufish spawning aggregation site in summer.

name: Carnac Island Reef
notes: Small island reef between Garden Island and the mainland. Shallow ledges good for baldchin and pink snapper.

name: Shoalwater Reef (Shoalwater Bay)
notes: Inside Shoalwater Bay Marine Park. Limestone ledges with heavy bait fish schools; good pink snapper on evening tides.

name: Penguin Island Reef
notes: Shallow reef around Penguin Island. Light tackle and herring fishing popular; good flathead on the sandy margins.

name: Five Fathom Bank
notes: Shallow sand bank approximately 9m deep (5 fathoms). Major bait school attractor; excellent KGW and flathead during autumn.

name: Eight Mile Reef (Rockingham)
notes: Offshore reef approximately 8 nautical miles from Rockingham. Productive bottom structure for demersal species.

name: Becher Point Reef
notes: Shallow inshore reef south of Rockingham. Good pink snapper and herring during the cooler months.

name: South West Reef (Mandurah)
notes: Offshore reef south-west of Mandurah. Deeper ledges hold good dhufish; fish rising tide for best results.

name: Bouvard Reef
notes: Limestone reef off Bouvard/Lake Clifton area. Productive demersal structure with good tailor surface action in summer.

name: Warnbro Sound Reef
notes: Inshore limestone reef inside Warnbro Sound. Sheltered water makes this a reliable option in strong sea breezes.

name: Jurien Bay Reef
notes: Extensive limestone reef system within Jurien Bay Marine Park. Pinnacles and ledges hold excellent demersal species.

name: Cervantes Reef
notes: Limestone reef off Cervantes. Less pressure than Jurien Bay reefs; good dhufish in 20–28m on deeper ledges.

name: Abrolhos Islands (Wallabi Group)
notes: Northernmost Abrolhos group. Coral platform with channels and drop-offs; prime baldchin groper habitat. Seasonal access only.

name: Abrolhos Islands (Easter Group)
notes: Middle Abrolhos group. World-class fishing destination for demersal species. Coral bommies and channels in 8–20m.

name: Abrolhos Islands (Pelsaert Group)
notes: Southernmost Abrolhos group. More exposed than northern groups; strong currents on drop-offs concentrate pelagics.

name: Batavia Wreck Reef (Abrolhos)
notes: Shallow reef near the famous Batavia wreck site (1629). Historical significance; fish the reef margins in 8–12m.

name: Geraldton Reef (Dongara Bank)
notes: Offshore reef/bank structure off the Geraldton coast. Good dhufish on the deeper ledges and bank edges.

name: Ningaloo Reef (Exmouth)
notes: World Heritage coral reef. The outer wall drops steeply to 500m+; giant trevally and Spanish mackerel patrol the current edges.

name: Ningaloo Reef (Coral Bay)
notes: Accessible coral reef with snorkelling and fishing from small boats. Channels between bommies hold coral trout and spangled emperor.

name: Ningaloo Reef (Tantabiddi)
notes: Northern Ningaloo boat ramp access point. Exposed outer reef with strong current edges; best on the tide changes.

name: Rowley Shoals (Imperieuse Reef)
notes: Remote offshore coral atoll 260km from Broome. World-class fishing; the passes run extremely fast on tides — time your fishing accordingly.

name: Rowley Shoals (Mermaid Reef)
notes: Northernmost Rowley Shoal. Large passes with exceptional tidal flow; dogtooth tuna and GT on the drop-offs.

name: Rowley Shoals (Clerke Reef)
notes: Middle Rowley Shoal. Similar to Imperieuse; fish the pass edges on the run-in tide for large predators.

name: Scott Reef (South)
notes: Extremely remote offshore reef near Timor Sea. Pristine fishing; requires live-aboard or large offshore vessel.

name: Scott Reef (North)
notes: North Scott Reef. Lagoon entrance channel famous for giant trevally ambush feeding on tidal flows.

name: Cartier Island Reef
notes: Isolated coral platform in the Timor Sea. Nature reserve; check access restrictions before fishing.

name: Ashmore Reef
notes: Marine park in the Timor Sea. Strict access regulations; permit required for any fishing activities.

name: Dampier Archipelago Reef
notes: Rock/reef complex among the Dampier Archipelago islands. Tide-driven current edges between islands hold large pelagics.

name: Montebello Islands Reef
notes: Remote coral island group. Exceptional fishing; access by live-aboard or Onslow charter. Best on tide changes.

name: Barrow Island Reef
notes: Class A nature reserve — no recreational fishing permitted. Listed for reference only.

name: Mackerel Islands Reef
notes: Island resort with excellent fishing. Spanish mackerel very reliable off the reef points on the run-in tide.

name: Point Cloates Reef
notes: Transition zone between tropical and temperate species. Exceptional species diversity; both tropical and demersal species present.

name: Shark Bay (Herald Bight)
notes: Reef structure inside Shark Bay World Heritage Area. Check local fishing regulations; some areas have restrictions.

name: Cocos Ledge (Fremantle)
notes: Deep limestone ledge system west of Fremantle. One of Perth's premier dhufish locations; fish the tide run with pilchards on ganged hooks.

name: Stragglers Reef (Fremantle)
notes: Ledge reef north-west of Fremantle. Consistent dhufish producer; best results on overcast days with northerly swell.

name: Roe Reef (Cockburn Sound)
notes: Shallow limestone reef inside Cockburn Sound. Sheltered water; good snapper fishing on evening tides.

name: Parker Point Reef (Rottnest)
notes: South-east Rottnest reef with overhangs and ledges. Dhufish hold under the limestone overhangs; burley up for best results.

name: Busselton Reef
notes: Limestone reef off Busselton in Geographe Bay. Productive for dhufish and samson fish in 15–25m.

name: Cape Naturaliste Reef
notes: Exposed cape reef with strong tidal currents. Samson fish and dhufish on the deeper edges; albacore in the current lines offshore.

name: Esperance Archipelago Reef
notes: Granite island reef complex unique to the south coast. Spectacular underwater scenery; dhufish and pink snapper on the deeper ledges.

name: Albany Reef (King George Sound)
notes: Granite reef inside King George Sound. Sheltered from prevailing swells; excellent dhufish and blue morwong in 15–30m.

name: Breaksea Island Reef
notes: Exposed outer island at the entrance to King George Sound. Strong currents on the ledges; albacore tuna and samson fish offshore.

name: Middle Reef (Garden Island)
notes: Limestone reef between Garden Island and the mainland. Reliable snapper spot on evening tides; herring school heavily on the reef.

name: Flathead Bank (Garden Island)
notes: Sandy bank off the eastern side of Garden Island. Named for its flathead fishing; also productive for KGW on outgoing tides.

name: Success Bank
notes: Shallow sand bank between Fremantle and Garden Island. KGW and herring concentrate over the sand; skipjack tuna hunt bait schools on the surface.

name: Perth Canyon (Head)
notes: Head of the Perth Canyon submarine canyon, 50km offshore. One of WA's premier offshore big-game locations; yellowfin and marlin aggregate at the canyon walls.

name: Perth Offshore Ledge (40–55m)
notes: The 40–55m depth contour shelf ledge off Perth. Prime dhufish ground; fish with fresh bait on the tide run.

name: HMAS Ovens Wreck
notes: Ex-HMAS Ovens submarine sunk as an artificial reef off Fremantle in 1997. Fish the wreck edges at dawn; dhufish and samson congregate around the structure.

name: Cockburn Sound Seagrass Flats
notes: Extensive seagrass meadows in Cockburn Sound. Prime KGW habitat; drift soft plastics over the weed beds on an outgoing tide.

name: Warnbro Sound Seagrass
notes: Shallow seagrass beds inside Warnbro Sound. Very sheltered fishing; KGW and herring year-round on prawns or worms.

name: Garden Island Kelp Zone
notes: Dense kelp forest on the western side of Garden Island. Tailor and herring school through the kelp; dhufish hunt the weed edges.

name: Marmion Lagoon Seagrass
notes: Seagrass meadows in the Marmion Lagoon. Good herring and KGW fishing from the beach or kayak; flounder at night.

name: Rockingham Rubble Ground
notes: Broken limestone rubble ground off Rockingham. Transition zone between sand and reef; baldchin groper patrol the rubble edges.

name: Lancelin Limestone Inshore
notes: Inshore limestone reef off Lancelin. Light recreational fishing pressure; good pink snapper on ledges in 12–18m.

name: Guineas (Offshore Pinnacles)
notes: Isolated limestone pinnacles 20km offshore. Upwellings concentrate baitfish and attract samson fish; exceptional jigging and bait fishing.

name: HMAS Swan Wreck
notes: Ex-HMAS Swan destroyer sunk as an artificial reef in 1997 near Dunsborough. Popular dive site; fish the wreck edges for dhufish and samson at dawn.

name: Cape Naturaliste Drop-off
notes: Steep limestone ledge off Cape Naturaliste. Samson fish and dhufish on the deep edges; albacore tuna in blue-water lines offshore.

name: Geographe Bay Sand Flats
notes: Sheltered sand flats in Geographe Bay. Excellent KGW fishing on light gear with worms or prawns; minimal swell inside the bay.

name: Busselton Jetty Reef
notes: Artificial reef structure built around the famous 1.8km Busselton Jetty. Snapper and tailor aggregate under the jetty pylons.

name: Margaret River Kelp Forest
notes: Southern temperate kelp forest off the Margaret River coast. Blue morwong and dhufish shelter in the kelp; good in calm conditions.

name: Augusta Canyon Approach
notes: Shelf edge off Cape Leeuwin where the Southern Ocean meets the Indian Ocean. Very productive when seas allow; albacore tuna in the current interface.

name: Albany Kelp Forest (Two Peoples Bay)
notes: Dense kelp forest around the Two Peoples Bay nature reserve. Blue morwong and salmon school through the kelp; dhufish on the outer rocky edges.

name: Bremer Bay Canyon (The Knob)
notes: Head of the Bremer Bay submarine canyon. WA's most famous big-game location with orca and sperm whale activity; January–April best for yellowfin tuna.

name: Esperance Granite Boulders
notes: Massive granite boulder fields unique to the south coast. Cave and ledge habitat in complex 3D structure; fish slowly with live bait.

name: Lucky Bay Sand Flats
notes: Crystal clear sand flats inside Cape Le Grand National Park. Excellent KGW sight-fishing in calm conditions; very clean water.

name: Abrolhos Channels & Gutters
notes: Fast-flowing tidal channels between Abrolhos coral platforms. Baldchin groper and dhufish aggregate in spawning season; match the hatch with live prawns.

name: Abrolhos Sand Flats
notes: Sandy inter-reef areas in the Abrolhos. KGW reach exceptional sizes here; drift with fresh prawn or squid over the sand patches.

name: Jurien Bay Seagrass
notes: Seagrass meadows inside Jurien Bay. Good KGW and herring fishing; land-based and kayak access from the bay beach.

name: Green Head Pinnacles
notes: Isolated limestone pinnacles off Green Head. Lesser-known spot with light fishing pressure; excellent dhufish on vertical jigs.

name: Shark Bay Seagrass Meadows
notes: World Heritage seagrass meadows — among the largest on earth. Excellent whiting and flathead fishing; care required for dugong and turtle habitat.

name: Exmouth Gulf Tidal Flats
notes: Shallow tidal sand flats in Exmouth Gulf. Queenfish and golden trevally chase baitfish on the flats at dawn; barramundi in the mangrove creeks.

name: Ningaloo Lagoon Sand
notes: Sand patches inside the Ningaloo Lagoon between the reef and shore. Trevally patrol the sand at dusk; queenfish blitzing bait schools at first light.

name: Dampier Offshore Shoals
notes: Submerged shoals offshore from Dampier port. Red emperor and coral trout on the harder bottom; Spanish mackerel in the current lines above.

name: Broome Offshore Banks
notes: Offshore bank structure north-west of Broome. Strong tidal currents over the banks concentrate fish; best on the tide run with fresh bait.

name: Kimberley Offshore Reef
notes: Remote Kimberley reef system. Pristine and rarely fished; extreme tidal range (up to 12m) drives extraordinary bait and predator aggregations.
